title: Westcoast Niggaz
lyrics:

Compton, South Central and Watts
Inglewood

You can stop tryna clone us west coast niggas
(DJ Skee, D-D-DJ Skee)

West Coast
You can't clone us west coast niggas
So stop tryna clone us west coast niggas
You can't clone us west coast niggas
You can stop tryna clone us west coast niggas
Uh

We missed Master Spade, had the hoes at the Snooty Fox
Nowa' days, they wanna wear white teas and dark shades
Nigga, you ain't tryna block no sun rays
Matter fact, your flag hanging out the wrong way
You ain''t never popped not swap meet] tags
You wouldn't know about nickels and dime bags the chronic
To Snoop said it in Chronic, I'm just tryna be honest
You took our slang and underlined it
Put it away and prayed to god we wouldn't find it, we not blinded
We see you niggas
Again, I'm seein' thouse niggas a long time ago too
It's time to air these niggas out
Uh
You can't clone us west coast niggas
(DJ Skee, Westside exclus-)
So stop tryna clone us west coast niggas
You can't clone us west coast niggas
You can stop tryna clone us west coast niggas
Stop it, uh
Look, we some west coast niggas for real
We the only ones, you can't clone us chump
So stop it, same old shit, but a different day
Same gas, same clip, '64 Chevrolet
Nigga welcome to that gang bang murder state
And if you perpetrate, slugs through your vertebrae
Then it's too late for apoligies
44 spit out shells like polis seeds
You doin' it wrong, that ain't me homie
My flag on the right side, that shit ain't crease on me
We taking our shit back, with interest, nigga
We started this G shit, we can end this motherfucker
Hey yo chop
Big'll take next, the beach cruiser
Let me catch 'em, Imma take they shit and put it on flat
Motherfucker
You can't clone us west coast niggas
So stop tryna clone us west coast niggas
You can't clone us west coast niggas
You can stop tryna clone us west coast niggas
Hey nigga, you got your motherfucking red rag in the fucking wrong side, man
That's the Snoop Dogg side, nigga, and that ain't a blue rag
It's cool if you're tryna to bang and shit, man,but  you just gotta wear that shit in the right side, nigga
Imma tell Snoop on your ass, nigga

ai_analysis.structure: The song primarily consists of a repeated chorus interspersed with several rap verses.  There's no distinct bridge section. The structure can be described as Chorus-Verse-Chorus-Verse-Chorus-Verse-Chorus.
ai_analysis.rhyme_scheme: The rhyme scheme is predominantly AABB within the verses, with the chorus repeating the same four lines.  The internal rhyme within the verses is also frequent, contributing to the song's rhythmic flow.  Overall, it's a simple, repetitive scheme that emphasizes the song's message.
ai_analysis.complexity_level: Medium. While the vocabulary isn't overly complex, the lyrics contain numerous references to gang culture, specific locations (Compton, Watts, Inglewood), and slang terms that require some familiarity with West Coast hip-hop culture to fully understand. The imagery is vivid and aggressive.
main_themes: Territoriality and pride in one's origins
ai_analysis.emotional_tone: Aggressive, defiant, assertive, and proud. There's a strong sense of anger and frustration at being imitated, coupled with a boastful display of the artists' identity and lifestyle.
ai_analysis.storytelling_type: Conversational and assertive. The song doesn't tell a linear narrative story but rather presents a forceful statement and a warning against imitation. It's a direct address to those perceived as copying the West Coast hip-hop style.
ai_analysis.wordplay_quality: Good. The song utilizes repetition effectively to emphasize its message.  While there aren't complex metaphors or extended similes, the use of slang, imagery related to gang culture (e.g., '40 ounce spiller', 'red and blue rag showers'), and the consistent repetition of 'You can't clone us' creates a strong and memorable impact.

title: Perfect Love (Short Version)
lyrics:
I know I am looking for a perfect love
Eyes open, eyes open for sure
I been lookin', I been lookin', peephole at the door
Hoping that the mailman could leave a box with a stamp
So I can mail my heart to you, shit you don't even know, come on
Eyes open, eyes open for sure
I been lookin', I been lookin', peephole at the door
Hoping that the mailman could leave a box with a stamp
So I can mail my heart to you, shit you don't even know, come on (Took my grills out)
I had a queen who I was with for about eight years
We bought each other matching grills, nobody's swag came near
And a mean Harlem joint who gеt drunk all the time
She say, "Hit it from bеhind while I sip some wine"
And my first? She was in a Ralph Lauren pageant
R.I.P Tennille, I never dreamed you'd be passin'
I'm a ghetto bitch, rich bitch, movie star assassin
Still a fucker, B-list model with wack fashion
Had a freak Houston chick that love to lick the gooch (Oop)
Fresh tattoos because the BBL is new
Redhead Brazilian with her freckles in the hoops
I was fuckin' a Parisian in a little Benz coupe (Vroom)
And we drive to Novo in Malibu, she workin' out
Got me runnin' up sand dunes
And I ain't gotta wear a mask like MF DOOM
You gotta love me for me, I'm just livin' my truth
(The perfect love)
Eyes open, eyes open for sure
I been lookin', I been lookin', peephole at the door (I've been searchin')
Hopin' that the mailman could leave a box with a stamp (For the perfect love)
So I can mail my heart to you, shit you don't even know, come on
Eyes open, eyes open for sure (I've been lookin')
I been lookin', I been lookin', peephole at the door
Hoping that the mailman could leave a box with a stamp
So I can mail my heart to you, shit you don't even know, come on (Yeah)
I had a girl from Inglewood who used to pop at the lips
And hated Red because her brother was Crip
Crazy shit, I had this geek girl who used to read tales about secret worlds
She said she loved the way I eat girls, wasn't hype enough
Had a skater boy with some issues, he liked to fight too much
His cover-up girlfriend, naw, didn't like me much, damn
Another bite the dust, she used me just to pipe them up
But when it's time to chill, they pipe down like aqueducts
So I got telescopes, x-rays, microscopes, weather far close
I could know if it's been anybody that's sittin' right beside me
The rear view is clear too, if they sit behind me
I'm tryna stunt, need a team like a Mike Robb pimpin'
I'm on the hunt, like a white blonde missin'
Can't even front, dawg, I like tongue kissin'
Shit, it's hella tight (Woo), I need an extra light (Woo)
It's gettin' dim out here, that's why I keep my eyes open
Eyes open, eyes open for sure (I've been lookin')
I been lookin', I been lookin', peephole at the door (I've been seachin')
Hoping that the mailman could leave a box with a stamp
So I can mail my heart to you, shit you don't even know, come on (For the perfect love)
Eyes open, eyes open for sure
I been lookin', I been lookin', peephole at the door (I've been lookin')
Hoping that the mailman could leave a box with a stamp (I've been searchin')
So I can mail my heart to you, shit you don't even know, come on (For the perfect love)
I been looking, I been searching
For the perfect love
I been looking
(I know I am looking for a perfect love)

ai_analysis.structure: The song follows a verse-chorus structure.  Multiple verses detail the singer's past relationships, interspersed with a repetitive chorus expressing the ongoing search for perfect love.
ai_analysis.rhyme_scheme: The rhyme scheme is primarily AABB within each verse, with the chorus having a consistent rhyme scheme as well.  However, the verses don't follow a consistent pattern relative to each other, making the overall rhyme scheme irregular.
ai_analysis.complexity_level: Medium. The language is relatively straightforward, but the imagery is vivid and the references to various women and experiences add a layer of complexity.
ai_analysis.emotional_tone: Mixed. The song conveys a blend of longing, vulnerability, and a degree of cynicism or world-weariness alongside moments of playful boasting.
ai_analysis.storytelling_type: Conversational and narrative. The song uses a conversational tone to recount a series of past relationships, creating a narrative arc of searching for love.
ai_analysis.wordplay_quality: Good.  The song uses some effective wordplay, particularly in the repeated chorus imagery of mailing one's heart and the contrasting descriptions of the various women encountered.  The use of slang and vivid descriptions enhances the wordplay.
